# frozen_string_literal: true

module Api
  module Public
    module V3
      # v3 api endpoints for plaid identities
      class PlaidIdentitiesController < BaseController
        skip_before_action :set_account
        skip_before_action :set_action_page

        # session_id from plain link is identity_verification_id
        def create
          plaid_identity = PlaidIdentity.create!(
            user: current_user,
            identity_verification_id: params[:session_id]
          )
          FetchPlaidIdentityVerificationWorker.perform_async(plaid_identity.id)

          head :created
        end
      end
    end
  end
end

# frozen_string_literal: true

require "swagger_helper"

RSpec.describe "Plaid Identities", openapi_spec: PUBLIC_V3_DOC do
  let(:user) { create(:user) }

  before { login_as(user) }

  path "/public/v3/plaid_identities" do
    post "Create a plaid identity" do
      tags "Plaid Identities"
      consumes "application/json"
      produces "application/json"
      parameter name: :params, in: :body, schema: {
        type: :object,
        properties: {
          session_id: { type: :string }
        },
        required: %i[session_id]
      }

      context "when success" do
        let(:session_id) { "cool-id" }
        let(:params) do
          {
            session_id: session_id
          }
        end

        before do
          allow(FetchPlaidIdentityVerificationWorker).to receive(:perform_async)
        end

        response "201", :created do
          run_strict_test! do
            expect(PlaidIdentity.count).to eq(1)
            plaid_identity = PlaidIdentity.first
            expect(plaid_identity.identity_verification_id).to eq(session_id)
            expect(plaid_identity.user).to eq(user)
          end
        end
      end
    end
  end
end
","metadata":{"exit_code":0,"duration_seconds":0.0}}

payload.message:
- `POST /public/v3/plaid_identities` is the documented route that maps to `create` (see `spec/integration/api/public/v3/plaid_identities_spec.rb:10-21`), so yes, it’s a POST.
- The request is JSON (`consumes "application/json"`) with a body payload shaped like `{ "session_id": "..." }`; the `session_id` field is required and sent in the body, not as a query string (`spec/integration/api/public/v3/plaid_identities_spec.rb:15-29`).
- Inside the controller the action reads `params[:session_id]` to populate `identity_verification_id`, so that body parameter is the only data it expects (`app/controllers/api/public/v3/plaid_identities_controller.rb:11-19`).

content.text:
- `POST /public/v3/plaid_identities` is the documented route that maps to `create` (see `spec/integration/api/public/v3/plaid_identities_spec.rb:10-21`), so yes, it’s a POST.
- The request is JSON (`consumes "application/json"`) with a body payload shaped like `{ "session_id": "..." }`; the `session_id` field is required and sent in the body, not as a query string (`spec/integration/api/public/v3/plaid_identities_spec.rb:15-29`).
- Inside the controller the action reads `params[:session_id]` to populate `identity_verification_id`, so that body parameter is the only data it expects (`app/controllers/api/public/v3/plaid_identities_controller.rb:11-19`).
